title: Genetic Markers as Coordinates of Population Motion
author: Lesperance, Joel Michael
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Genetic markers such as the Rh (Rhesus) blood group are often discussed or received as indicators of ancestral purity or fixed biological identity. This paper advances a complementary framing: low-dimensional genetic markers function as coordinates of population motion, reflecting differential rates of admixture, isolation, and continuity over time. Rather than encoding static ancestral states, these markers preserve signals of relative persistence under constrained gene flow. This framing aligns population genetics with the inherently non-stationary nature of living systems and clarifies how biology, culture, geography, and time jointly shape human genetic landscapes.
